report_name: T-Mobile Confirms Insider Data Breach, States Only One Customer Affected

threat_actors.name: Vendor Employee
threat_actors.description: An employee of a third-party vendor for T-Mobile who improperly accessed the account information of a single customer, exposing PII including name, address, account PIN, and Social Security Number.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: A vendor employee abused their legitimate access privileges to view and potentially exfiltrate a customer's sensitive account data.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Information Repositories
attack_patterns.description: A vendor employee accessed and exposed sensitive Personally Identifiable Information (PII) for one individual from T-Mobile's customer account database.

identities.name: T-Mobile USA
identities.description: A US-based telecommunications company that experienced an insider data breach where a third-party vendor employee improperly accessed a customer's account information.

indicators.name: Anomalous Vendor Account Access on Customer Records
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Vendor Account Access on Customer Records
id: 2b9c1a8e-5f0d-4b3a-8e7f-9c1d2e3f4a5b
status: experimental
description: Detects access to customer records by vendor accounts. This activity could be legitimate but should be monitored for insider threat activity, such as accessing an unusual number of records or records outside of a normal workflow.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/t-mobile-sets-the-record-straight-on-latest-data-breach-filing/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-03
tags:
  - attack.t1078
  - attack.t1213
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate access by vendor employees performing their job functions.
level: medium
logsource:
  category: application
  product: crm
detection:
  selection:
    user.name|startswith: 'vendor_'
    event.action: 'customer_record_accessed'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Monitors for customer service or vendor accounts accessing customer records, which could indicate anomalous behavior associated with an insider threat. This is based on the report's recommendation to use UEBA to baseline normal access patterns.

relationships.source_name: Anomalous Vendor Account Access on Customer Records
